claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start the assessment, I need to ask you a few questions.

**First, let me ask about your child:**

How old is your child (or the youngest child if you have more than one)? I need to know if they are:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16-19 years old
- 20 or over

This helps determine the basic eligibility criteria, as Child Benefit rules differ depending on the child's age.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi, thanks for helping me with this! 

I actually have three children I'm asking about - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 10. So they're all under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful! Thank you for that information. You have three children - Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (10) - all under 16, which is great.

Since all three children are under 16, they generally meet the age requirement. Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Next question:**

Are any of your children working less than 24 hours per week, or are they not in paid work at all? 

(Given their ages - 3, 8, and 10 - I assume they're not in paid work, but I need to confirm this to proceed correctly through the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are working - they're just kids! Alex is only a toddler, and Blake and Charlie are still in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that makes sense!

**Next question:**

Are any of your children receiving certain benefits in their own right, such as:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit
- Or similar benefits claimed in their own name?

(Again, given their young ages, I expect the answer is no, but I need to check this as part of the eligibility criteria.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them get any benefits in their own names. They're just children - I'm the one who handles all that sort of thing for the family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, that's what I expected.

**Next question:**

Are any of your children in an apprenticeship program? This includes:
- Any type of apprenticeship in England (Intermediate, Advanced, Higher, or Degree Apprenticeships)
- Modern Apprenticeships in Scotland
- Apprenticeships in Wales (except Foundation level, which are approved)

(Given that Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 10, and they're in primary school/toddler age, I assume none are in apprenticeships, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - they're way too young for that! Like I said, they're just in primary school or still at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now we're moving on to questions about you as the claimant.

**Residency Requirements:**

Do you meet the UK residency requirements? You qualify if **ANY** of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (most common)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself and family (without claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do you meet any of these residency requirements?** Most people qualify simply by normally living in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester with the kids.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! You meet the residency requirement by normally living in the UK.

**Responsibility for the Children:**

Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie? You're considered responsible if **ANY** of the following apply:

1. **You live with the children** (most common)
2. You contribute to their upkeep by paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

Based on what you've told me - that you live in Manchester with the kids - it sounds like you live with them. Can you confirm that you live with Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, I live with Alex and Charlie - they're at home with me. But Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that! So Alex and Charlie live with you, but Blake doesn't.

**For Blake (who doesn't live with you):**

Even though Blake doesn't live with you, you can still be considered responsible if you contribute to Blake's upkeep. This means:

- You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be (or equivalent in kind) towards Blake's care
- This can include contributions like: food, clothes, pocket money, housing costs, presents, etc.
- These can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments
- OR you're paying maintenance under a court order or agreement

**Do you contribute financially to Blake's upkeep?** If so, can you tell me a bit more about that arrangement?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing anything financially for Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being honest about that. Let me make sure I understand your situation correctly:

- **Alex (age 3)**: Lives with you ✓
- **Blake (age 8)**: Does NOT live with you, and you do NOT contribute financially to their upkeep
- **Charlie (age 10)**: Lives with you ✓

Based on the Child Benefit rules, you would be considered responsible for Alex and Charlie (since they live with you), but **not responsible for Blake** (since Blake doesn't live with you and you're not contributing to their upkeep).

This means you would likely be eligible for Child Benefit for **Alex and Charlie**, but **not for Blake**.

Let me continue with a few more questions about Alex and Charlie to complete the assessment. 

**Next question:**

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie? For example, another parent, a grandparent, or another family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! No one else is claiming for them.

**Next question - about where the children are living:**

For Alex and Charlie, are either of them currently:
- In hospital for an extended period?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?
- Or are they living normally with you at home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're both living normally at home with me - no hospital stays or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just one more question:

**Final question:**

Is the local council paying towards Alex's or Charlie's accommodation or maintenance? 

(Generally, you cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child's care or accommodation.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything for them - I'm supporting them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 📋 Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment Complete

Based on the information you've provided, here's your eligibility outcome:

---

### ✅ **ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie**
### ❌ **NOT ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit for Blake**

---

### **Individual Assessment:**

**Alex (age 3): ✅ ELIGIBLE**
- Alex meets all criteria: under 16, lives with you, not in paid work, not receiving benefits, no one else claiming, living normally at home, and council not paying for their care.

**Blake (age 8): ❌ NOT ELIGIBLE** 
- While Blake meets the age requirement, you are not considered "responsible" for Blake under Child Benefit rules because Blake doesn't live with you AND you don't contribute financially to their upkeep. The responsibility requirement is essential for claiming Child Benefit.

**Charlie (age 10): ✅ ELIGIBLE**
- Charlie meets all criteria: under 16, lives with you, not in paid work, not receiving benefits, no one else claiming, living normally at home, and council not paying for their care.

---

### **Next Steps for Alex and Charlie:**

1. **Apply for Child Benefit** - You can apply online or by post using form CH2
2. **Provide documentation:**
   - Proof of your identity
   - Proof of the children's identity
   - Birth certificates for Alex and Charlie
3. **Be aware:** If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge
4. **Payments:** You'll receive payments every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

**Apply here:** https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

---

### **About Blake:**

If someone else is responsible for Blake (living with them or contributing to their upkeep), that person may be able to claim Child Benefit for Blake instead.

Do you have any questions about this assessment?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) is eligible for Child Benefit. Alex is under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex by living with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Alex is living normally at home, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake (age 8) is NOT eligible for Child Benefit under this claimant. While Blake is under 16 years old and meets the basic age requirement, the claimant is not considered responsible for Blake. Blake does not live with the claimant, and the claimant does not contribute financially to Blake's upkeep through maintenance payments or other contributions. The responsibility requirement is not met, which is a fundamental criterion for Child Benefit eligibility.

Charlie (age 10) is eligible for Child Benefit. Charlie is under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Charlie by living with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, Charlie is living normally at home, and the local council is not paying towards Charlie's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in Manchester with two of their three children (Alex and Charlie) and meets all UK residency requirements. For the two children living with them (Alex age 3 and Charlie age 10), all eligibility criteria are satisfied including responsibility, no duplicate claims, normal living arrangements, and no council support. However, for Blake (age 8), who does not live with the claimant and for whom the claimant provides no financial contribution, the responsibility criterion is not met, making the claimant ineligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 3) is eligible for Child Benefit. Alex is under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex by living with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Alex is living normally at home, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake (age 8) is NOT eligible for Child Benefit under this claimant. While Blake is under 16 years old and meets the basic age requirement, the claimant is not considered responsible for Blake. Blake does not live with the claimant, and the claimant does not contribute financially to Blake's upkeep through maintenance payments or other contributions. The responsibility requirement is not met, which is a fundamental criterion for Child Benefit eligibility.

Charlie (age 10) is eligible for Child Benefit. Charlie is under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Charlie by living with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, Charlie is living normally at home, and the local council is not paying towards Charlie's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in Manchester with two of their three children (Alex and Charlie) and meets all UK residency requirements. For the two children living with them (Alex age 3 and Charlie age 10), all eligibility criteria are satisfied including responsibility, no duplicate claims, normal living arrangements, and no council support. However, for Blake (age 8), who does not live with the claimant and for whom the claimant provides no financial contribution, the responsibility criterion is not met, making the claimant ineligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
